These cannot both be true without a more precise differentiating claim, making this a structural logical consistency failure.","R1.4":"The novelty delta vs. JudgeStream is underspecified. If JudgeStream already pioneered inline residualization, LenSlice's distinct contribution (length-specific decile routing? end-to-end reference pool management?) must be sharply carved out. 'Improve' almost certainly requires resolving this.","R1.6":"'Improve this draft' centrally implies prose quality — sentence clarity, paragraph cohesion, concision, and register consistency across the four paragraphs.","R1.7":"The JudgeStream citation is directly implicated in the logical consistency and novelty issues; how that prior work is characterized (what it did vs. did not do) determines whether the 'first system' claim survives, making citation accuracy load-relevant here."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the improvement be limited to prose and writing quality (sentence clarity, concision, flow), or should it also include substantive revisions to logical consistency and novelty positioning — specifically resolving the apparent contradiction between LenSlice's 'first system' assertion and the JudgeStream prior work that 'pioneered inline residualization for LLM judges'?"}

rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim ('first end-to-end system to make length debiasing a property of the serving layer') and gap statement ('existing pipelines treat debiasing as post-hoc') are the load-bearing framing elements; improvement may require sharpening or revising both.
rationale.R1.2: There is a direct internal contradiction: the draft simultaneously claims LenSlice is 'first' to make debiasing a serving-layer property and credits JudgeStream (2023) with having 'pioneered inline residualization for LLM judges on identical hardware.' These cannot both be true without a more precise differentiating claim, making this a structural logical consistency failure.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty delta vs. JudgeStream is underspecified. If JudgeStream already pioneered inline residualization, LenSlice's distinct contribution (length-specific decile routing? end-to-end reference pool management?) must be sharply carved out. 'Improve' almost certainly requires resolving this.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' centrally implies prose quality — sentence clarity, paragraph cohesion, concision, and register consistency across the four paragraphs.
rationale.R1.7: The JudgeStream citation is directly implicated in the logical consistency and novelty issues; how that prior work is characterized (what it did vs. did not do) determines whether the 'first system' claim survives, making citation accuracy load-relevant here.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement be limited to prose and writing quality (sentence clarity, concision, flow), or should it also include substantive revisions to logical consistency and novelty positioning — specifically resolving the apparent contradiction between LenSlice's 'first system' assertion and the JudgeStream prior work that 'pioneered inline residualization for LLM judges'?
